comments.author: @hualani6785
comments.text: WTH?? While Ukraine is indeed a beautiful nation, this video ideologue is SO full of BS! Both Coffee and Cacao are beans that grow on bushes (shorter trees), and DO NOT grow underground Lviv!. Like all trees, these beans need sunshine, soil and rain to grow and mature. IF anyone is selling tours with hard hats underground, its lies for tourism. Do a bit lf research, dont buy into total bunk.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your thoughts, Iryna. Stories of justice and survival always carry a lot of emotion, especially in places shaped by struggle. What part of Ukraine’s story do you feel people around the world still don’t understand enough?

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: Ukraine honestly feels like one of those places where reality is much bigger than the headlines. Ancient cities, golden churches, vast countryside, and resilient communities somehow remind us that a country is more than the stories that make the news. History there seems to have a very long memory.

comments.author: @trueunrealglobe
comments.text: This documentary beautifully captured the essence of exploration and reminded me how much of the world remains undiscovered.

comments.author: @NomadicCorner
comments.text: This video gave me a completely different perspective on this country.

comments.author: @ChanelNatureTravel4K
comments.text: Ukraine feels full of contrasts, quiet landscapes carrying one of the most intense histories in Europe.

comments.text: Funny how The West propaganda always tries to portray Ukraine as a totally different entity to Russia...

replies.author: @ОлександрОлефір-щ7з
replies.text: And why do you think that Ukraine is Russia? Can you even find Russia on the map? Not the All-Russian Empire, not the Russian Federative Soviet Republic, not the Russian Federation, but the land of the Russian ethnic group. Find Russia in which the Russian ethnic group was historically formed? Around Moscow, the lands of the Finno-Ugric peoples, immediately to the east and south - the lands of nomadic ethnic groups, the inhabitants of Novgorod and Pskov - you exterminated to zero in the Middle Ages. Show the land of the Russians as a Slavic ethnic group, to which the lands of other ethnic groups were annexed? You will not find it, because Russia is a story invented in the 18th century, with mythical Rurikovichs. Russky is the name of the concession of Orthodox people subordinate to the Kyivan (Ruthenian) Patriarchate, which later became Moscow. And only since 1937, by Stalin's decision, a nation appeared - Russian. Before that, they were called Muscovites, then Great Russians. But not Russian. You live in a fictional world in which the slave population (Russians) flaunts fictional power and victories. However, slaves will not defeat free people. And Ukraine has never been Russia, and will never become Russia.
